noveltyJustification: 4 wholly new findings (P0-4 race quantification, P0-5 unconditional PID write, P0-6 zero worker spawns, P1-2 in-flight task close) + 1 partially new (P2-2 recovery checklist building on iter-1 P0-2)

title: Race window between _pid_alive and bind is 5-60ms; structurally unrecoverable without cross-process lock
recommendation: Add fcntl.flock(daemon.pid, LOCK_EX|LOCK_NB) in start_daemon() (primary) + daemon-side PID re-check after Listener bind (secondary). Spec's 'race window acceptable' stance must be removed.

title: daemon.py:568 pid_path.write_text is unconditional; orphans previous daemon's PID
recommendation: Move PID write inside the lock-held region (P0-4 fix). Open with O_CREAT|O_EXCL or flock+truncate.

title: No multiprocessing.Process/Pool/SemLock anywhere in daemon code; PID 40681 resource_tracker is from transitive dep
recommendation: Spec.md does NOT need worker-reaping logic. Q5 scope can shrink. Contradicts iter-1 framing of Q5.

title: _async_daemon_main shutdown gather() has no per-task timeout
recommendation: Wrap gather in wait_for(timeout=Xs) with cancel+close fallback. Lower priority than P0 stack.

title: Recovery snippet incomplete: missing 'stop callers first' and 'verify no remaining processes' steps
recommendation: Replace with 6-step checklist (stop callers, pkill, pgrep verify, ls state, restart, status verify).
